1	import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
2	import {
3	  addTodo,
4	  deleteTodo,
5	  expectTodoActive,
6	  expectTodoCompleted,
7	  expectTodoVisible,
8	  getTodoItem,
9	  toggleTodo,
10	} from '../fixtures/test-helpers'
11	
12	function uniqueText(base: string) {
13	  return `${base}-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 6)}`
14	}
15	
16	async function createAndWaitForTodo(page: import('@playwright/test').Page, text: string) {
17	  const responsePromise = page.waitForResponse(
18	    (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos') && response.status() === 201,
19	  )
20	  await addTodo(page, text)
21	  await responsePromise
22	}
23	
24	test.describe('Journey 2: Returning User — Task Lifecycle', () => {
25	  test.describe('Complete a task', () => {
26	    test('clicking a task marks it as completed with visual distinction', async ({ page }) => {
27	      await page.goto('/')
28	      const taskText = uniqueText('Complete me')
29	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
30	
31	      // Verify active state first
32	      await expectTodoActive(page, taskText)
33	
34	      // Click to complete
35	      const patchPromise = page.waitForResponse(
36	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
37	      )
38	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
39	      await patchPromise
40	
41	      // Verify completed state — strikethrough + italic + dimmed color
42	      await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)
43	    })
44	
45	    test('completed state persists after page refresh', async ({ page }) => {
46	      await page.goto('/')
47	      const taskText = uniqueText('Persist complete')
48	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
49	
50	      const patchPromise = page.waitForResponse(
51	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
52	      )
53	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
54	      await patchPromise
55	      await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)
56	
57	      // Refresh and verify persistence
58	      await page.reload()
59	      await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)
60	    })
61	  })
62	
63	  test.describe('Uncomplete a task', () => {
64	    test('clicking a completed task reverts it to active visual state', async ({ page }) => {
65	      await page.goto('/')
66	      const taskText = uniqueText('Uncomplete me')
67	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
68	
69	      // Complete first
70	      const patchPromise1 = page.waitForResponse(
71	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
72	      )
73	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
74	      await patchPromise1
75	      await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)
76	
77	      // Uncomplete
78	      const patchPromise2 = page.waitForResponse(
79	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
80	      )
81	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
82	      await patchPromise2
83	
84	      // Verify back to active state
85	      await expectTodoActive(page, taskText)
86	    })
87	
88	    test('uncompleted (active) state persists after page refresh', async ({ page }) => {
89	      await page.goto('/')
90	      const taskText = uniqueText('Persist active')
91	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
92	
93	      // Complete then uncomplete
94	      const patchPromise1 = page.waitForResponse(
95	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
96	      )
97	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
98	      await patchPromise1
99	
100	      const patchPromise2 = page.waitForResponse(
101	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
102	      )
103	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
104	      await patchPromise2
105	      await expectTodoActive(page, taskText)
106	
107	      // Refresh and verify persistence
108	      await page.reload()
109	      await expectTodoActive(page, taskText)
110	    })
111	  })
112	
113	  test.describe('Delete a task', () => {
114	    test('clicking × removes the task from the list', async ({ page }) => {
115	      await page.goto('/')
116	      const taskText = uniqueText('Delete me')
117	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
118	      await expectTodoVisible(page, taskText)
119	
120	      // Delete via × button
121	      const deletePromise = page.waitForResponse(
122	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'DELETE',
123	      )
124	      await deleteTodo(page, taskText)
125	      await deletePromise
126	
127	      // Task should be gone
128	      await expect(page.getByText(taskText)).not.toBeVisible()
129	    })
130	
131	    test('deleted task is permanently gone after page refresh', async ({ page }) => {
132	      await page.goto('/')
133	      const taskText = uniqueText('Gone forever')
134	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
135	
136	      const deletePromise = page.waitForResponse(
137	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'DELETE',
138	      )
139	      await deleteTodo(page, taskText)
140	      await deletePromise
141	      await expect(page.getByText(taskText)).not.toBeVisible()
142	
143	      // Refresh and confirm permanent deletion
144	      await page.reload()
145	      await expect(page.getByText(taskText)).not.toBeVisible()
146	    })
147	
148	    test('deletion is immediate — no confirmation dialog', async ({ page }) => {
149	      await page.goto('/')
150	      const taskText = uniqueText('No confirm')
151	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
152	
153	      // Click × and verify no dialog appears
154	      let dialogAppeared = false
155	      page.on('dialog', () => { dialogAppeared = true })
156	
157	      const deletePromise = page.waitForResponse(
158	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'DELETE',
159	      )
160	      await deleteTodo(page, taskText)
161	      await deletePromise
162	
163	      expect(dialogAppeared).toBe(false)
164	      await expect(page.getByText(taskText)).not.toBeVisible()
165	    })
166	  })
167	
168	  test.describe('Full lifecycle flow', () => {
169	    test('create, complete, uncomplete, and delete in sequence', async ({ page }) => {
170	      await page.goto('/')
171	      const taskText = uniqueText('Full lifecycle')
172	
173	      // Create
174	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
175	      await expectTodoActive(page, taskText)
176	
177	      // Complete
178	      const patchPromise1 = page.waitForResponse(
179	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
180	      )
181	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
182	      await patchPromise1
183	      await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)
184	
185	      // Uncomplete
186	      const patchPromise2 = page.waitForResponse(
187	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
188	      )
189	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
190	      await patchPromise2
191	      await expectTodoActive(page, taskText)
192	
193	      // Delete
194	      const deletePromise = page.waitForResponse(
195	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'DELETE',
196	      )
197	      await deleteTodo(page, taskText)
198	      await deletePromise
199	      await expect(page.getByText(taskText)).not.toBeVisible()
200	
201	      // Verify gone after refresh
202	      await page.reload()
203	      await expect(page.getByText(taskText)).not.toBeVisible()
204	    })
205	  })
206	})
207

content.thinking:
The E2E tests were already committed (`965dbd4 Story: tests increase the e2e coverage`). Now let me analyze the epics ACs for Story 3.5 against the existing Journey 2 tests.

**Epics ACs for Story 3.5:**
1. Complete a task → strikethrough + italic + dimmed + persist after refresh
2. Uncomplete a task → active visual state + persist after refresh
3. Delete via × → disappears + persist (gone) after refresh
4. Journey 2 lifecycle scenarios pass

**Existing Journey 2 tests coverage:**
1. AC1: "clicking a task marks it as completed with visual distinction" + "completed state persists after page refresh" ✅


2. AC2: "clicking a completed task reverts it to active visual state" + "uncompleted state persists after refresh" ✅
3. AC3: "clicking × removes the task from the list" + "deleted task permanently gone after refresh" + "deletion is immediate — no confirmation" ✅
4. AC4: "create, complete, uncomplete, and delete in sequence" (full lifecycle) ✅

All four ACs are covered, but there's a gap: AC1 specifically requires strikethrough, italic, and dimmed color styling, while the current test helper only validates the `aria-checked` attribute without checking the actual CSS classes that apply those visual styles.

In E2E testing though, checking CSS classes directly is brittle—`aria-checked` is the semantic truth that accessibility tools rely on, and the visual styling is already validated at the unit level. The real question is whether to note this gap in the story or add a visual assertion test. Looking back at AC1, it explicitly calls out the visual presentation, so I should document this discrepancy and consider whether a visual check belongs in the E2E suite.

input.content:
# Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests

Status: ready-for-dev

## Story

As a user,
I want to complete, uncomplete, and delete tasks in a full end-to-end flow,
So that the entire task lifecycle is verified across the full stack.

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)

1. **Given** the full stack is running with existing tasks
   **When** a user completes a task
   **Then** the task shows strikethrough + italic + dimmed color
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the completed state

2. **Given** a completed task exists
   **When** a user clicks the task to uncomplete it
   **Then** the task returns to active visual state
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the active state

3. **Given** a task exists
   **When** a user deletes it via the `×` button
   **Then** the task disappears from the list
   **And** refreshing the page confirms the task is permanently gone

4. **Given** the E2E tests are run
   **When** the returning user journey (Journey 2) is executed
   **Then** all lifecycle scenarios (complete, uncomplete, delete, persistence) pass

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Verify existing Journey 2 E2E tests cover all ACs (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [ ] 1.1: Run `journey-returning-user.spec.ts` and confirm all 8 tests pass
  - [ ] 1.2: Cross-reference each AC against test assertions — document coverage
  - [ ] 1.3: Identify any AC gaps not covered by existing tests

- [ ] Task 2: Add visual assertion for completed state if missing (AC: #1)
  - [ ] 2.1: Verify `expectTodoCompleted` checks `aria-checked="true"` (semantic completion)
  - [ ] 2.2: Assess whether CSS visual assertions (strikethrough, italic, dimmed) are needed at E2E level or sufficiently covered by component tests
  - [ ] 2.3: If gap exists, add CSS class assertion to E2E helper; if component tests cover it, document rationale

- [ ] Task 3: Run full E2E regression suite (AC: #4)
  - [ ] 3.1: Run all Playwright tests — Journey 1 + Journey 2 — verify 17/17 pass
  - [ ] 3.2: Run frontend unit test suite — verify 59/59 pass

## Dev Notes

### CRITICAL: Tests Already Exist

The QA agent (Quinn) generated the Journey 2 E2E tests during this sprint session. The tests are:
- **File:** `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` (8 tests)
- **Helpers added:** `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` (+5 new helpers)
- **Status:** Already committed (`965dbd4`), all 17/17 E2E tests passing

**This story is a verification and gap-fill story, NOT a write-from-scratch story.** The dev agent should:
1. Verify existing tests map to ACs
2. Fill any gaps
3. Run full regression

### Existing E2E Test → AC Mapping

| Test | AC |
|------|-----|
| `clicking a task marks it as completed with visual distinction` | AC1 |
| `completed state persists after page refresh` | AC1 |
| `clicking a completed task reverts it to active visual state` | AC2 |
| `uncompleted (active) state persists after page refresh` | AC2 |
| `clicking × removes the task from the list` | AC3 |
| `deleted task is permanently gone after page refresh` | AC3 |
| `deletion is immediate — no confirmation dialog` | AC3 (bonus) |
| `create, complete, uncomplete, and delete in sequence` | AC4 |

### Visual Assertion Gap Analysis

AC1 says "strikethrough + italic + dimmed color" — the existing `expectTodoCompleted` helper checks `aria-checked="true"` which is the **semantic** completion signal. The **visual** CSS classes (`line-through italic text-text-secondary`) are already tested in component tests (`TodoItem.test.tsx` lines 38-43). E2E tests typically assert on user-visible outcomes, not implementation details. The `aria-checked` attribute is the accessibility-level truth of completion state.

**Recommended approach:** Document that visual CSS is tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level. No additional visual assertions needed unless the reviewer disagrees.

### Test Helpers Available

From `e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts`:
- `addTodo(page, text)` — fill input + press Enter
- `getTodoItem(page, text)` — locate by `role="checkbox"` + text filter
- `toggleTodo(page, text)` — click the todo item
- `deleteTodo(page, text)` — click × via `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"`
- `expectTodoCompleted(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="true"`
- `expectTodoActive(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="false"`
- `expectTodoVisible(page, text)` — assert visible
- `getTodoCount(page)` — count items
- `getTodoTextAtPosition(page, position)` — get text at index

### Files to VERIFY (already exist)

| File | Status |
|------|--------|
| `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` | Already committed, 8 tests |
| `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` | Already committed, 5 new helpers |

### Files NOT to touch

- All backend files — E2E-only story
- All frontend source files — no component changes
- Frontend unit test files — no changes needed
- `journey-first-time-user.spec.ts` — Journey 1, no changes

### Previous Story Intelligence

**From Story 3.4 (Task Deletion UI):**
- Frontend tests: 59/59 passing
- Delete × button uses `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"` — used by E2E `deleteTodo` helper
- Toggle uses `role="checkbox"` with `aria-checked` — used by E2E `expectTodoCompleted/Active`

**From QA Automation (Quinn):**
- Generated 8 Journey 2 tests covering all lifecycle scenarios
- All tests use `waitForResponse` to ensure API calls complete before assertions
- Uses `uniqueText()` pattern to avoid test data collisions
- E2E total: 17 tests (9 Journey 1 + 8 Journey 2)

### Git Intelligence

**Commit pattern:** `feat: Story X.Y: Description`
**Expected commit:** `feat: Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests`
**Recent:** `965dbd4` already committed QA-generated tests

### Scope Boundaries

- This is an **E2E test verification story** — no feature implementation
- Do NOT modify frontend or backend source code
- Do NOT add tests for Epic 4 features (empty state, loading, errors) — that's Story 4.5
- Do NOT add accessibility E2E tests — that's Story 5.4

### Library & Framework Requirements

| Library | Version | Usage |
|---------|---------|-------|
| `@playwright/test` | v1.58.x | E2E test framework |

**IMPORTANT:** Do NOT install any new dependencies.

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.5] — acceptance criteria (4 ACs, verified 1:1)
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Journey 2] — returning user journey scenarios
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Testing] — Playwright E2E covering all user journeys
- [Source: bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts] — existing Journey 2 tests (8 tests)
- [Source: bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts] — shared test helpers
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/tests/test-summary.md] — QA automation summary

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

### Completion Notes List

### Change Log

### File List

# Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests

Status: ready-for-dev

## Story

As a user,
I want to complete, uncomplete, and delete tasks in a full end-to-end flow,
So that the entire task lifecycle is verified across the full stack.

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)

1. **Given** the full stack is running with existing tasks
   **When** a user completes a task
   **Then** the task shows strikethrough + italic + dimmed color
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the completed state

2. **Given** a completed task exists
   **When** a user clicks the task to uncomplete it
   **Then** the task returns to active visual state
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the active state

3. **Given** a task exists
   **When** a user deletes it via the `×` button
   **Then** the task disappears from the list
   **And** refreshing the page confirms the task is permanently gone

4. **Given** the E2E tests are run
   **When** the returning user journey (Journey 2) is executed
   **Then** all lifecycle scenarios (complete, uncomplete, delete, persistence) pass

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Verify existing Journey 2 E2E tests cover all ACs (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [ ] 1.1: Run `journey-returning-user.spec.ts` and confirm all 8 tests pass
  - [ ] 1.2: Cross-reference each AC against test assertions — document coverage
  - [ ] 1.3: Identify any AC gaps not covered by existing tests

- [ ] Task 2: Add visual assertion for completed state if missing (AC: #1)
  - [ ] 2.1: Verify `expectTodoCompleted` checks `aria-checked="true"` (semantic completion)
  - [ ] 2.2: Assess whether CSS visual assertions (strikethrough, italic, dimmed) are needed at E2E level or sufficiently covered by component tests
  - [ ] 2.3: If gap exists, add CSS class assertion to E2E helper; if component tests cover it, document rationale

- [ ] Task 3: Run full E2E regression suite (AC: #4)
  - [ ] 3.1: Run all Playwright tests — Journey 1 + Journey 2 — verify 17/17 pass
  - [ ] 3.2: Run frontend unit test suite — verify 59/59 pass

## Dev Notes

### CRITICAL: Tests Already Exist

The QA agent (Quinn) generated the Journey 2 E2E tests during this sprint session. The tests are:
- **File:** `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` (8 tests)
- **Helpers added:** `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` (+5 new helpers)
- **Status:** Already committed (`965dbd4`), all 17/17 E2E tests passing

**This story is a verification and gap-fill story, NOT a write-from-scratch story.** The dev agent should:
1. Verify existing tests map to ACs
2. Fill any gaps
3. Run full regression

### Existing E2E Test → AC Mapping

| Test | AC |
|------|-----|
| `clicking a task marks it as completed with visual distinction` | AC1 |
| `completed state persists after page refresh` | AC1 |
| `clicking a completed task reverts it to active visual state` | AC2 |
| `uncompleted (active) state persists after page refresh` | AC2 |
| `clicking × removes the task from the list` | AC3 |
| `deleted task is permanently gone after page refresh` | AC3 |
| `deletion is immediate — no confirmation dialog` | AC3 (bonus) |
| `create, complete, uncomplete, and delete in sequence` | AC4 |

### Visual Assertion Gap Analysis

AC1 says "strikethrough + italic + dimmed color" — the existing `expectTodoCompleted` helper checks `aria-checked="true"` which is the **semantic** completion signal. The **visual** CSS classes (`line-through italic text-text-secondary`) are already tested in component tests (`TodoItem.test.tsx` lines 38-43). E2E tests typically assert on user-visible outcomes, not implementation details. The `aria-checked` attribute is the accessibility-level truth of completion state.

**Recommended approach:** Document that visual CSS is tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level. No additional visual assertions needed unless the reviewer disagrees.

### Test Helpers Available

From `e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts`:
- `addTodo(page, text)` — fill input + press Enter
- `getTodoItem(page, text)` — locate by `role="checkbox"` + text filter
- `toggleTodo(page, text)` — click the todo item
- `deleteTodo(page, text)` — click × via `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"`
- `expectTodoCompleted(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="true"`
- `expectTodoActive(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="false"`
- `expectTodoVisible(page, text)` — assert visible
- `getTodoCount(page)` — count items
- `getTodoTextAtPosition(page, position)` — get text at index

### Files to VERIFY (already exist)

| File | Status |
|------|--------|
| `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` | Already committed, 8 tests |
| `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` | Already committed, 5 new helpers |

### Files NOT to touch

- All backend files — E2E-only story
- All frontend source files — no component changes
- Frontend unit test files — no changes needed
- `journey-first-time-user.spec.ts` — Journey 1, no changes

### Previous Story Intelligence

**From Story 3.4 (Task Deletion UI):**
- Frontend tests: 59/59 passing
- Delete × button uses `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"` — used by E2E `deleteTodo` helper
- Toggle uses `role="checkbox"` with `aria-checked` — used by E2E `expectTodoCompleted/Active`

**From QA Automation (Quinn):**
- Generated 8 Journey 2 tests covering all lifecycle scenarios
- All tests use `waitForResponse` to ensure API calls complete before assertions
- Uses `uniqueText()` pattern to avoid test data collisions
- E2E total: 17 tests (9 Journey 1 + 8 Journey 2)

### Git Intelligence

**Commit pattern:** `feat: Story X.Y: Description`
**Expected commit:** `feat: Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests`
**Recent:** `965dbd4` already committed QA-generated tests

### Scope Boundaries

- This is an **E2E test verification story** — no feature implementation
- Do NOT modify frontend or backend source code
- Do NOT add tests for Epic 4 features (empty state, loading, errors) — that's Story 4.5
- Do NOT add accessibility E2E tests — that's Story 5.4

### Library & Framework Requirements

| Library | Version | Usage |
|---------|---------|-------|
| `@playwright/test` | v1.58.x | E2E test framework |

**IMPORTANT:** Do NOT install any new dependencies.

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.5] — acceptance criteria (4 ACs, verified 1:1)
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Journey 2] — returning user journey scenarios

- [Source: bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts] — shared test helpers
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/tests/test-summary.md] — QA automation summary

**8/8 pass.** Subtask 1.1 confirmed.

### Subtask 1.2 — Cross-reference each AC against test assertions

Let me read the actual test assertions to verify coverage:

content.content: Wasted call — file unchanged since your last Read. Refer to that earlier tool_result instead.

content.text:
File in context. Cross-referencing:

**AC1: Complete → strikethrough + italic + dimmed + persist**
- Test `clicking a task marks it as completed with visual distinction` (line 26): Creates task → verifies active (`aria-checked=false`) → toggles via PATCH → verifies completed (`aria-checked=true`) ✅
- Test `completed state persists after page refresh` (line 45): Toggles → `expectTodoCompleted` → `page.reload()` → `expectTodoCompleted` again ✅
- **Visual gap:** `aria-checked` is semantic. Strikethrough/italic/dimmed are CSS. Component tests cover CSS classes. Documented.

**AC2: Uncomplete → active visual + persist**
- Test `clicking a completed task reverts it to active visual state` (line 64): Complete → uncomplete via PATCH → `expectTodoActive` ✅
- Test `uncompleted state persists after page refresh` (line 88): Complete → uncomplete → reload → `expectTodoActive` ✅

**AC3: Delete via × → gone + persist**
- Test `clicking × removes the task from the list` (line 114): Create → delete via × → `not.toBeVisible()` ✅
- Test `deleted task is permanently gone after page refresh` (line 131): Delete → reload → `not.toBeVisible()` ✅
- Test `deletion is immediate — no confirmation dialog` (line 148): Bonus — verifies no dialog ✅

**AC4: Journey 2 lifecycle pass**
- Test `create, complete, uncomplete, and delete in sequence` (line 169): Full lifecycle in one flow → create → complete → uncomplete → delete → reload verify ✅

### Subtask 1.3 — Identify AC gaps

**Gap identified:** AC1 says "strikethrough + italic + dimmed color." The E2E tests verify `aria-checked="true"` (semantic) but don't directly assert CSS visual properties. However:
- Component tests (`TodoItem.test.tsx` lines 38-43) already assert `text-text-secondary`, `line-through`, `italic` CSS classes
- E2E best practice: assert user-visible outcomes, not implementation details
- `aria-checked` is the accessibility-level truth that drives the visual state

**Decision:** No gap requiring E2E-level CSS assertions. The layered testing strategy (component → CSS classes, E2E → semantic state + persistence) provides full coverage.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests

Status: ready-for-dev

## Story

As a user,
I want to complete, uncomplete, and delete tasks in a full end-to-end flow,
So that the entire task lifecycle is verified across the full stack.

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)

1. **Given** the full stack is running with existing tasks
   **When** a user completes a task
   **Then** the task shows strikethrough + italic + dimmed color
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the completed state

2. **Given** a completed task exists
   **When** a user clicks the task to uncomplete it
   **Then** the task returns to active visual state
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the active state

3. **Given** a task exists
   **When** a user deletes it via the `×` button
   **Then** the task disappears from the list
   **And** refreshing the page confirms the task is permanently gone

4. **Given** the E2E tests are run
   **When** the returning user journey (Journey 2) is executed
   **Then** all lifecycle scenarios (complete, uncomplete, delete, persistence) pass

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [ ] Task 1: Verify existing Journey 2 E2E tests cover all ACs (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [ ] 1.1: Run `journey-returning-user.spec.ts` and confirm all 8 tests pass
  - [ ] 1.2: Cross-reference each AC against test assertions — document coverage
  - [ ] 1.3: Identify any AC gaps not covered by existing tests

- [ ] Task 2: Add visual assertion for completed state if missing (AC: #1)
  - [ ] 2.1: Verify `expectTodoCompleted` checks `aria-checked="true"` (semantic completion)
  - [ ] 2.2: Assess whether CSS visual assertions (strikethrough, italic, dimmed) are needed at E2E level or sufficiently covered by component tests
  - [ ] 2.3: If gap exists, add CSS class assertion to E2E helper; if component tests cover it, document rationale

- [ ] Task 3: Run full E2E regression suite (AC: #4)
  - [ ] 3.1: Run all Playwright tests — Journey 1 + Journey 2 — verify 17/17 pass
  - [ ] 3.2: Run frontend unit test suite — verify 59/59 pass

## Dev Notes

### CRITICAL: Tests Already Exist

The QA agent (Quinn) generated the Journey 2 E2E tests during this sprint session. The tests are:
- **File:** `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` (8 tests)
- **Helpers added:** `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` (+5 new helpers)
- **Status:** Already committed (`965dbd4`), all 17/17 E2E tests passing

**This story is a verification and gap-fill story, NOT a write-from-scratch story.** The dev agent should:
1. Verify existing tests map to ACs
2. Fill any gaps
3. Run full regression

### Existing E2E Test → AC Mapping

| Test | AC |
|------|-----|
| `clicking a task marks it as completed with visual distinction` | AC1 |
| `completed state persists after page refresh` | AC1 |
| `clicking a completed task reverts it to active visual state` | AC2 |
| `uncompleted (active) state persists after page refresh` | AC2 |
| `clicking × removes the task from the list` | AC3 |
| `deleted task is permanently gone after page refresh` | AC3 |
| `deletion is immediate — no confirmation dialog` | AC3 (bonus) |
| `create, complete, uncomplete, and delete in sequence` | AC4 |

### Visual Assertion Gap Analysis

AC1 says "strikethrough + italic + dimmed color" — the existing `expectTodoCompleted` helper checks `aria-checked="true"` which is the **semantic** completion signal. The **visual** CSS classes (`line-through italic text-text-secondary`) are already tested in component tests (`TodoItem.test.tsx` lines 38-43). E2E tests typically assert on user-visible outcomes, not implementation details. The `aria-checked` attribute is the accessibility-level truth of completion state.

**Recommended approach:** Document that visual CSS is tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level. No additional visual assertions needed unless the reviewer disagrees.

### Test Helpers Available

From `e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts`:
- `addTodo(page, text)` — fill input + press Enter
- `getTodoItem(page, text)` — locate by `role="checkbox"` + text filter
- `toggleTodo(page, text)` — click the todo item
- `deleteTodo(page, text)` — click × via `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"`
- `expectTodoCompleted(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="true"`
- `expectTodoActive(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="false"`
- `expectTodoVisible(page, text)` — assert visible
- `getTodoCount(page)` — count items
- `getTodoTextAtPosition(page, position)` — get text at index

### Files to VERIFY (already exist)

| File | Status |
|------|--------|
| `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` | Already committed, 8 tests |
| `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` | Already committed, 5 new helpers |

### Files NOT to touch

- All backend files — E2E-only story
- All frontend source files — no component changes
- Frontend unit test files — no changes needed
- `journey-first-time-user.spec.ts` — Journey 1, no changes

### Previous Story Intelligence

**From Story 3.4 (Task Deletion UI):**
- Frontend tests: 59/59 passing
- Delete × button uses `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"` — used by E2E `deleteTodo` helper
- Toggle uses `role="checkbox"` with `aria-checked` — used by E2E `expectTodoCompleted/Active`

**From QA Automation (Quinn):**
- Generated 8 Journey 2 tests covering all lifecycle scenarios
- All tests use `waitForResponse` to ensure API calls complete before assertions
- Uses `uniqueText()` pattern to avoid test data collisions
- E2E total: 17 tests (9 Journey 1 + 8 Journey 2)

### Git Intelligence

**Commit pattern:** `feat: Story X.Y: Description`
**Expected commit:** `feat: Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests`
**Recent:** `965dbd4` already committed QA-generated tests

### Scope Boundaries

- This is an **E2E test verification story** — no feature implementation
- Do NOT modify frontend or backend source code
- Do NOT add tests for Epic 4 features (empty state, loading, errors) — that's Story 4.5
- Do NOT add accessibility E2E tests — that's Story 5.4

### Library & Framework Requirements

| Library | Version | Usage |
|---------|---------|-------|
| `@playwright/test` | v1.58.x | E2E test framework |

**IMPORTANT:** Do NOT install any new dependencies.

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.5] — acceptance criteria (4 ACs, verified 1:1)
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Journey 2] — returning user journey scenarios
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Testing] — Playwright E2E covering all user journeys
- [Source: bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts] — existing Journey 2 tests (8 tests)
- [Source: bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts] — shared test helpers
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/tests/test-summary.md] — QA automation summary

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

### Debug Log References

### Completion Notes List

### Change Log

### File List

## Task 2: Visual assertion assessment (AC: #1)

### Subtask 2.1 — Verify `expectTodoCompleted` checks semantic completion

The helper at `test-helpers.ts:38-41`:
```typescript
export async function expectTodoCompleted(page: Page, text: string) {
  const item = getTodoItem(page, text)
  await expect(item).toHaveAttribute('aria-checked', 'true')
}
```
Confirmed: checks `aria-checked="true"` (semantic). ✅

### Subtask 2.2 — Assess CSS visual assertions need

Component-level coverage in `TodoItem.test.tsx:38-43`:
```typescript
it('renders completed todo with strikethrough and italic in secondary color', () => {
  // ... asserts text-text-secondary, line-through, italic
})
```

**Assessment:** The testing pyramid is correct:
- **Component tests** verify CSS class application (`line-through`, `italic`, `text-text-secondary`)
- **E2E tests** verify semantic state (`aria-checked`) and persistence across refresh
- Adding CSS class checks at E2E level would duplicate component coverage and create brittle tests tied to Tailwind class names

### Subtask 2.3 — Decision: No additional E2E CSS assertions needed

Rationale documented. Component tests cover visual CSS; E2E tests cover semantic state + full-stack persistence. Layered strategy is sound.

# Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests

Status: ready-for-dev

## Story

As a user,
I want to complete, uncomplete, and delete tasks in a full end-to-end flow,
So that the entire task lifecycle is verified across the full stack.

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)

1. **Given** the full stack is running with existing tasks
   **When** a user completes a task
   **Then** the task shows strikethrough + italic + dimmed color
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the completed state

2. **Given** a completed task exists
   **When** a user clicks the task to uncomplete it
   **Then** the task returns to active visual state
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the active state

3. **Given** a task exists
   **When** a user deletes it via the `×` button
   **Then** the task disappears from the list
   **And** refreshing the page confirms the task is permanently gone

4. **Given** the E2E tests are run
   **When** the returning user journey (Journey 2) is executed
   **Then** all lifecycle scenarios (complete, uncomplete, delete, persistence) pass

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Verify existing Journey 2 E2E tests cover all ACs (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [x] 1.1: Run `journey-returning-user.spec.ts` and confirm all 8 tests pass — 8/8 pass
  - [x] 1.2: Cross-reference each AC against test assertions — all 4 ACs mapped to 8 tests
  - [x] 1.3: Identify any AC gaps — visual CSS tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level; no gap requiring additional E2E assertions

- [ ] Task 2: Add visual assertion for completed state if missing (AC: #1)
  - [ ] 2.1: Verify `expectTodoCompleted` checks `aria-checked="true"` (semantic completion)
  - [ ] 2.2: Assess whether CSS visual assertions (strikethrough, italic, dimmed) are needed at E2E level or sufficiently covered by component tests
  - [ ] 2.3: If gap exists, add CSS class assertion to E2E helper; if component tests cover it, document rationale

- [ ] Task 3: Run full E2E regression suite (AC: #4)
  - [ ] 3.1: Run all Playwright tests — Journey 1 + Journey 2 — verify 17/17 pass
  - [ ] 3.2: Run frontend unit test suite — verify 59/59 pass

## Dev Notes

### CRITICAL: Tests Already Exist

The QA agent (Quinn) generated the Journey 2 E2E tests during this sprint session. The tests are:
- **File:** `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` (8 tests)
- **Helpers added:** `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` (+5 new helpers)
- **Status:** Already committed (`965dbd4`), all 17/17 E2E tests passing

**This story is a verification and gap-fill story, NOT a write-from-scratch story.** The dev agent should:
1. Verify existing tests map to ACs
2. Fill any gaps
3. Run full regression

### Existing E2E Test → AC Mapping

| Test | AC |
|------|-----|
| `clicking a task marks it as completed with visual distinction` | AC1 |
| `completed state persists after page refresh` | AC1 |
| `clicking a completed task reverts it to active visual state` | AC2 |
| `uncompleted (active) state persists after page refresh` | AC2 |
| `clicking × removes the task from the list` | AC3 |
| `deleted task is permanently gone after page refresh` | AC3 |
| `deletion is immediate — no confirmation dialog` | AC3 (bonus) |
| `create, complete, uncomplete, and delete in sequence` | AC4 |

### Visual Assertion Gap Analysis

AC1 says "strikethrough + italic + dimmed color" — the existing `expectTodoCompleted` helper checks `aria-checked="true"` which is the **semantic** completion signal. The **visual** CSS classes (`line-through italic text-text-secondary`) are already tested in component tests (`TodoItem.test.tsx` lines 38-43). E2E tests typically assert on user-visible outcomes, not implementation details. The `aria-checked` attribute is the accessibility-level truth of completion state.

**Recommended approach:** Document that visual CSS is tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level. No additional visual assertions needed unless the reviewer disagrees.

### Test Helpers Available

From `e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts`:
- `addTodo(page, text)` — fill input + press Enter
- `getTodoItem(page, text)` — locate by `role="checkbox"` + text filter
- `toggleTodo(page, text)` — click the todo item
- `deleteTodo(page, text)` — click × via `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"`
- `expectTodoCompleted(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="true"`
- `expectTodoActive(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="false"`
- `expectTodoVisible(page, text)` — assert visible
- `getTodoCount(page)` — count items
- `getTodoTextAtPosition(page, position)` — get text at index

### Files to VERIFY (already exist)

| File | Status |
|------|--------|
| `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` | Already committed, 8 tests |
| `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` | Already committed, 5 new helpers |

### Files NOT to touch

- All backend files — E2E-only story
- All frontend source files — no component changes
- Frontend unit test files — no changes needed
- `journey-first-time-user.spec.ts` — Journey 1, no changes

### Previous Story Intelligence

**From Story 3.4 (Task Deletion UI):**
- Frontend tests: 59/59 passing
- Delete × button uses `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"` — used by E2E `deleteTodo` helper
- Toggle uses `role="checkbox"` with `aria-checked` — used by E2E `expectTodoCompleted/Active`

**From QA Automation (Quinn):**
- Generated 8 Journey 2 tests covering all lifecycle scenarios
- All tests use `waitForResponse` to ensure API calls complete before assertions
- Uses `uniqueText()` pattern to avoid test data collisions
- E2E total: 17 tests (9 Journey 1 + 8 Journey 2)

### Git Intelligence

**Commit pattern:** `feat: Story X.Y: Description`
**Expected commit:** `feat: Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests`
**Recent:** `965dbd4` already committed QA-generated tests

### Scope Boundaries

- This is an **E2E test verification story** — no feature implementation
- Do NOT modify frontend or backend source code
- Do NOT add tests for Epic 4 features (empty state, loading, errors) — that's Story 4.5
- Do NOT add accessibility E2E tests — that's Story 5.4

### Library & Framework Requirements

| Library | Version | Usage |
|---------|---------|-------|
| `@playwright/test` | v1.58.x | E2E test framework |

**IMPORTANT:** Do NOT install any new dependencies.

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.5] — acceptance criteria (4 ACs, verified 1:1)
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Journey 2] — returning user journey scenarios
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Testing] — Playwright E2E covering all user journeys
- [Source: bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts] — existing Journey 2 tests (8 tests)
- [Source: bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts] — shared test helpers
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/tests/test-summary.md] — QA automation summary

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

### Debug Log References

### Completion Notes List

### Change Log

### File List

- [x] 1.3: Identify any AC gaps — visual CSS tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level; no gap requiring additional E2E assertions

content.thinking:
One test failed: "completed state persists after page refresh". This is likely a timing issue — the PATCH response came back, but the cache invalidation/refetch hadn't completed before the reload. Let me check the test more carefully.

The test at line 45:
```typescript
test('completed state persists after page refresh', async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto('/')
  const taskText = uniqueText('Persist complete')
  await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)


```

I'm looking at the test flow — it creates a todo, waits for the PATCH response when toggling completion, then reloads the page and expects the completed state to still be there. The failure is happening after the reload when checking if the todo is marked as completed, which suggests the server might not be persisting the state correctly or the client isn't refetching it properly on reload. race condition — the page reloaded but the todo list hasn't finished fetching from the API yet. The `expectTodoCompleted` tries to find the item immediately after reload, but the data might not be loaded yet.

Actually wait, the error happens at line 55 which is `await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)` BEFORE the reload (the first assertion). Let me re-read...

Line 55 in the test file is:
```
await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)
```
after `await patchPromise`.

This means the PATCH response came back, but the UI hasn't updated yet (cache invalidation → refetch → render is async). The test waits for the PATCH response but not for the UI to update.

The issue is that after the PATCH resolves, the mutation's `onSettled` callback invalidates the query and triggers a refetch, which then re-renders the component — but there's an async gap between the response and the UI actually updating. Playwright's `expect` assertions do auto-retry with a 5-second timeout, so `expectTodoCompleted` should theoretically wait for the `aria-checked` attribute to change. This could be a flaky test that occasionally times out, or there might be a timing issue with the server or database causing the refetch to take longer than expected.

content.text: One flaky failure — "completed state persists after page refresh." Playwright's auto-retry should handle this, but let me verify it's a flake by retrying:

# Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests

Status: ready-for-dev

## Story

As a user,
I want to complete, uncomplete, and delete tasks in a full end-to-end flow,
So that the entire task lifecycle is verified across the full stack.

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)

1. **Given** the full stack is running with existing tasks
   **When** a user completes a task
   **Then** the task shows strikethrough + italic + dimmed color
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the completed state

2. **Given** a completed task exists
   **When** a user clicks the task to uncomplete it
   **Then** the task returns to active visual state
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the active state

3. **Given** a task exists
   **When** a user deletes it via the `×` button
   **Then** the task disappears from the list
   **And** refreshing the page confirms the task is permanently gone

4. **Given** the E2E tests are run
   **When** the returning user journey (Journey 2) is executed
   **Then** all lifecycle scenarios (complete, uncomplete, delete, persistence) pass

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Verify existing Journey 2 E2E tests cover all ACs (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [x] 1.1: Run `journey-returning-user.spec.ts` and confirm all 8 tests pass — 8/8 pass
  - [x] 1.2: Cross-reference each AC against test assertions — all 4 ACs mapped to 8 tests
  - [x] 1.3: Identify any AC gaps — visual CSS tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level; no gap requiring additional E2E assertions

- [x] Task 2: Add visual assertion for completed state if missing (AC: #1)
  - [x] 2.1: Verify `expectTodoCompleted` checks `aria-checked="true"` (semantic completion) — confirmed
  - [x] 2.2: Assess whether CSS visual assertions needed — component tests cover CSS classes (line-through, italic, text-text-secondary); E2E covers semantic state + persistence
  - [x] 2.3: No additional E2E CSS assertions needed — layered testing strategy is sound, adding CSS checks would duplicate component coverage and create brittle tests

- [x] Task 3: Run full E2E regression suite (AC: #4)
  - [x] 3.1: Run all Playwright tests — Journey 1 + Journey 2 — 17/17 pass (one flake on first run, 17/17 on retry)
  - [x] 3.2: Run frontend unit test suite — 59/59 pass, zero regressions

## Dev Notes

### CRITICAL: Tests Already Exist

The QA agent (Quinn) generated the Journey 2 E2E tests during this sprint session. The tests are:
- **File:** `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` (8 tests)
- **Helpers added:** `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` (+5 new helpers)
- **Status:** Already committed (`965dbd4`), all 17/17 E2E tests passing

**This story is a verification and gap-fill story, NOT a write-from-scratch story.** The dev agent should:
1. Verify existing tests map to ACs
2. Fill any gaps
3. Run full regression

### Existing E2E Test → AC Mapping

| Test | AC |
|------|-----|
| `clicking a task marks it as completed with visual distinction` | AC1 |
| `completed state persists after page refresh` | AC1 |
| `clicking a completed task reverts it to active visual state` | AC2 |
| `uncompleted (active) state persists after page refresh` | AC2 |
| `clicking × removes the task from the list` | AC3 |
| `deleted task is permanently gone after page refresh` | AC3 |
| `deletion is immediate — no confirmation dialog` | AC3 (bonus) |
| `create, complete, uncomplete, and delete in sequence` | AC4 |

### Visual Assertion Gap Analysis

AC1 says "strikethrough + italic + dimmed color" — the existing `expectTodoCompleted` helper checks `aria-checked="true"` which is the **semantic** completion signal. The **visual** CSS classes (`line-through italic text-text-secondary`) are already tested in component tests (`TodoItem.test.tsx` lines 38-43). E2E tests typically assert on user-visible outcomes, not implementation details. The `aria-checked` attribute is the accessibility-level truth of completion state.

**Recommended approach:** Document that visual CSS is tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level. No additional visual assertions needed unless the reviewer disagrees.

### Test Helpers Available

From `e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts`:
- `addTodo(page, text)` — fill input + press Enter
- `getTodoItem(page, text)` — locate by `role="checkbox"` + text filter
- `toggleTodo(page, text)` — click the todo item
- `deleteTodo(page, text)` — click × via `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"`
- `expectTodoCompleted(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="true"`
- `expectTodoActive(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="false"`
- `expectTodoVisible(page, text)` — assert visible
- `getTodoCount(page)` — count items
- `getTodoTextAtPosition(page, position)` — get text at index

### Files to VERIFY (already exist)

| `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` | Already committed, 8 tests |
| `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` | Already committed, 5 new helpers |

### Files NOT to touch

- All backend files — E2E-only story
- All frontend source files — no component changes
- Frontend unit test files — no changes needed
- `journey-first-time-user.spec.ts` — Journey 1, no changes

### Previous Story Intelligence

**From Story 3.4 (Task Deletion UI):**
- Frontend tests: 59/59 passing
- Delete × button uses `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"` — used by E2E `deleteTodo` helper
- Toggle uses `role="checkbox"` with `aria-checked` — used by E2E `expectTodoCompleted/Active`

**From QA Automation (Quinn):**
- Generated 8 Journey 2 tests covering all lifecycle scenarios
- All tests use `waitForResponse` to ensure API calls complete before assertions
- Uses `uniqueText()` pattern to avoid test data collisions
- E2E total: 17 tests (9 Journey 1 + 8 Journey 2)

### Git Intelligence

- Do NOT add tests for Epic 4 features (empty state, loading, errors) — that's Story 4.5
- Do NOT add accessibility E2E tests — that's Story 5.4

   **And** refreshing the page preserves the active state

3. **Given** a task exists
   **When** a user deletes it via the `×` button
   **Then** the task disappears from the list
   **And** refreshing the page confirms the task is permanently gone

4. **Given** the E2E tests are run
   **When** the returning user journey (Journey 2) is executed
   **Then** all lifecycle scenarios (complete, uncomplete, delete, persistence) pass

- [x] Task 3: Run full E2E regression suite (AC: #4)

## Dev Notes

### CRITICAL: Tests Already Exist

The QA agent (Quinn) generated the Journey 2 E2E tests during this sprint session. The tests are:
- **File:** `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` (8 tests)

### Visual Assertion Gap Analysis

AC1 says "strikethrough + italic + dimmed color" — the existing `expectTodoCompleted` helper checks `aria-checked="true"` which is the **semantic** completion signal. The **visual** CSS classes (`line-through italic text-text-secondary`) are already tested in component tests (`TodoItem.test.tsx` lines 38-43). E2E tests typically assert on user-visible outcomes, not implementation details. The `aria-checked` attribute is the accessibility-level truth of completion state.

**Recommended approach:** Document that visual CSS is tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level. No additional visual assertions needed unless the reviewer disagrees.

- `expectTodoVisible(page, text)` — assert visible

- `getTodoTextAtPosition(page, position)` — get text at index

## Step 4 — Present findings

**🔥 CODE REVIEW FINDINGS, Brainrepo!**

**Story:** 3-5-task-lifecycle-e2e-tests
**Git vs Story Discrepancies:** 0
**Tests:** 17/17 E2E passing, 59/59 unit passing
**Issues Found:** 0 Critical, 0 High, 2 Medium, 1 Low

---

### 🟡 MEDIUM ISSUES

**M1: `createAndWaitForTodo` is defined inline instead of in shared helpers**
[journey-returning-user.spec.ts:16-22](bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts#L16-L22) — The `createAndWaitForTodo` helper (which wraps `addTodo` + `waitForResponse` for 201) is defined locally in this spec file. Journey 1 tests (`journey-first-time-user.spec.ts`) use the same pattern inline (e.g., lines 20-22, 38-40, 55-57). When Journey 3 is added (Story 4.5), it will need the same helper. This should live in `test-helpers.ts` to avoid duplication.
**Fix:** Move `createAndWaitForTodo` to `test-helpers.ts` and update both spec files to import it.

**M2: Flaky test not hardened — "completed state persists after page refresh"**
The dev-story documented a flake at line 45 where `expectTodoCompleted` fails after PATCH response but before cache invalidation completes. The fix relies on Playwright's auto-retry (5s default) and CI `retries: 2`. However, the root cause is that `waitForResponse(PATCH)` only confirms the server responded — it doesn't wait for TanStack Query's `onSettled` → `invalidateQueries` → refetch → re-render cycle. Adding a brief `await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)` wait *before* reload is correct (Playwright retries), but a more robust pattern is to also wait for the subsequent GET refetch response. This isn't critical (retries handle it), but it's a known weakness.
**Action:** Document as acceptable risk; or add `waitForResponse(GET /api/todos)` after the PATCH to ensure the refetch completes.

---

### 🟢 LOW ISSUES

**L1: `uniqueText` utility duplicated across spec files**
[journey-returning-user.spec.ts:12-14](bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts#L12-L14) and [journey-first-time-user.spec.ts:4-6](bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-first-time-user.spec.ts#L4-L6) — Same function defined in both files. Should be in `test-helpers.ts`.

---

### ✅ Verified Claims

- All 4 ACs mapped to 8 tests — **confirmed**
- Visual CSS assertions at component level (TodoItem.test.tsx:38-43: `line-through`, `italic`, `text-text-secondary`) — **confirmed**
- `aria-checked` as semantic E2E assertion — **correct approach**
- "No files created or modified" — **confirmed** (everything was committed at `965dbd4`)
- 17/17 E2E + 59/59 unit passing — **confirmed**

27	      await patchPromise
28	
29	      // Verify completed state — strikethrough + italic + dimmed color
30	      await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)
31	    })
32	
33	    test('completed state persists after page refresh', async ({ page }) => {
34	      await page.goto('/')
35	      const taskText = uniqueText('Persist complete')
36	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
37	
38	      const patchPromise = page.waitForResponse(
39	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
40	      )
41	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
42	      await patchPromise
43	      await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)
44	
45	      // Refresh and verify persistence
46	      await page.reload()
47	      await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)
48	    })
49	  })
50	
51	  test.describe('Uncomplete a task', () => {
52	    test('clicking a completed task reverts it to active visual state', async ({ page }) => {
53	      await page.goto('/')
54	      const taskText = uniqueText('Uncomplete me')
55	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
56	
57	      // Complete first
58	      const patchPromise1 = page.waitForResponse(
59	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
60	      )
61	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
62	      await patchPromise1
63	      await expectTodoCompleted(page, taskText)
64	
65	      // Uncomplete
66	      const patchPromise2 = page.waitForResponse(
67	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
68	      )
69	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
70	      await patchPromise2
71	
72	      // Verify back to active state
73	      await expectTodoActive(page, taskText)
74	    })
75	
76	    test('uncompleted (active) state persists after page refresh', async ({ page }) => {
77	      await page.goto('/')
78	      const taskText = uniqueText('Persist active')
79	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
80	
81	      // Complete then uncomplete
82	      const patchPromise1 = page.waitForResponse(
83	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
84	      )
85	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
86	      await patchPromise1
87	
88	      const patchPromise2 = page.waitForResponse(
89	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'PATCH',
90	      )
91	      await toggleTodo(page, taskText)
92	      await patchPromise2
93	      await expectTodoActive(page, taskText)
94	
95	      // Refresh and verify persistence
96	      await page.reload()
97	      await expectTodoActive(page, taskText)
98	    })
99	  })
100	
101	  test.describe('Delete a task', () => {
102	    test('clicking × removes the task from the list', async ({ page }) => {
103	      await page.goto('/')
104	      const taskText = uniqueText('Delete me')
105	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
106	      await expectTodoVisible(page, taskText)
107	
108	      // Delete via × button
109	      const deletePromise = page.waitForResponse(
110	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'DELETE',
111	      )
112	      await deleteTodo(page, taskText)
113	      await deletePromise
114	
115	      // Task should be gone
116	      await expect(page.getByText(taskText)).not.toBeVisible()
117	    })
118	
119	    test('deleted task is permanently gone after page refresh', async ({ page }) => {
120	      await page.goto('/')
121	      const taskText = uniqueText('Gone forever')
122	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)
123	
124	      const deletePromise = page.waitForResponse(
125	        (response) => response.url().includes('/api/todos/') && response.request().method() === 'DELETE',
126	      )
127	      await deleteTodo(page, taskText)
128	      await deletePromise
129	      await expect(page.getByText(taskText)).not.toBeVisible()
130	
131	      // Refresh and confirm permanent deletion
132	      await page.reload()
133	      await expect(page.getByText(taskText)).not.toBeVisible()
134	    })
135	
136	    test('deletion is immediate — no confirmation dialog', async ({ page }) => {
137	      await page.goto('/')

139	      await createAndWaitForTodo(page, taskText)

144	
145	      const deletePromise = page.waitForResponse(

147	      )

154	  })

168	      )

176	      )

180	
181	      // Delete

184	      )
185	      await deleteTodo(page, taskText)

The `expectTodoCompleted` assertion might run before the refetch completes and re-renders. I'll wait for the GET refetch response after PATCH in the two persistence tests:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests

Status: review

## Story

As a user,
I want to complete, uncomplete, and delete tasks in a full end-to-end flow,
So that the entire task lifecycle is verified across the full stack.

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)

1. **Given** the full stack is running with existing tasks
   **When** a user completes a task
   **Then** the task shows strikethrough + italic + dimmed color
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the completed state

2. **Given** a completed task exists
   **When** a user clicks the task to uncomplete it
   **Then** the task returns to active visual state
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the active state

3. **Given** a task exists
   **When** a user deletes it via the `×` button
   **Then** the task disappears from the list
   **And** refreshing the page confirms the task is permanently gone

4. **Given** the E2E tests are run
   **When** the returning user journey (Journey 2) is executed
   **Then** all lifecycle scenarios (complete, uncomplete, delete, persistence) pass

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Verify existing Journey 2 E2E tests cover all ACs (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [x] 1.1: Run `journey-returning-user.spec.ts` and confirm all 8 tests pass — 8/8 pass
  - [x] 1.2: Cross-reference each AC against test assertions — all 4 ACs mapped to 8 tests
  - [x] 1.3: Identify any AC gaps — visual CSS tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level; no gap requiring additional E2E assertions

- [x] Task 2: Add visual assertion for completed state if missing (AC: #1)
  - [x] 2.1: Verify `expectTodoCompleted` checks `aria-checked="true"` (semantic completion) — confirmed
  - [x] 2.2: Assess whether CSS visual assertions needed — component tests cover CSS classes (line-through, italic, text-text-secondary); E2E covers semantic state + persistence
  - [x] 2.3: No additional E2E CSS assertions needed — layered testing strategy is sound, adding CSS checks would duplicate component coverage and create brittle tests

- [x] Task 3: Run full E2E regression suite (AC: #4)
  - [x] 3.1: Run all Playwright tests — Journey 1 + Journey 2 — 17/17 pass (one flake on first run, 17/17 on retry)
  - [x] 3.2: Run frontend unit test suite — 59/59 pass, zero regressions

## Dev Notes

### CRITICAL: Tests Already Exist

The QA agent (Quinn) generated the Journey 2 E2E tests during this sprint session. The tests are:
- **File:** `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` (8 tests)
- **Helpers added:** `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` (+5 new helpers)
- **Status:** Already committed (`965dbd4`), all 17/17 E2E tests passing

**This story is a verification and gap-fill story, NOT a write-from-scratch story.** The dev agent should:
1. Verify existing tests map to ACs
2. Fill any gaps
3. Run full regression

### Existing E2E Test → AC Mapping

| Test | AC |
|------|-----|
| `clicking a task marks it as completed with visual distinction` | AC1 |
| `completed state persists after page refresh` | AC1 |
| `clicking a completed task reverts it to active visual state` | AC2 |
| `uncompleted (active) state persists after page refresh` | AC2 |
| `clicking × removes the task from the list` | AC3 |
| `deleted task is permanently gone after page refresh` | AC3 |
| `deletion is immediate — no confirmation dialog` | AC3 (bonus) |
| `create, complete, uncomplete, and delete in sequence` | AC4 |

### Visual Assertion Gap Analysis

AC1 says "strikethrough + italic + dimmed color" — the existing `expectTodoCompleted` helper checks `aria-checked="true"` which is the **semantic** completion signal. The **visual** CSS classes (`line-through italic text-text-secondary`) are already tested in component tests (`TodoItem.test.tsx` lines 38-43). E2E tests typically assert on user-visible outcomes, not implementation details. The `aria-checked` attribute is the accessibility-level truth of completion state.

**Recommended approach:** Document that visual CSS is tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level. No additional visual assertions needed unless the reviewer disagrees.

### Test Helpers Available

From `e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts`:
- `addTodo(page, text)` — fill input + press Enter
- `getTodoItem(page, text)` — locate by `role="checkbox"` + text filter
- `toggleTodo(page, text)` — click the todo item
- `deleteTodo(page, text)` — click × via `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"`
- `expectTodoCompleted(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="true"`
- `expectTodoActive(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="false"`
- `expectTodoVisible(page, text)` — assert visible
- `getTodoCount(page)` — count items
- `getTodoTextAtPosition(page, position)` — get text at index

### Files to VERIFY (already exist)

| File | Status |
|------|--------|
| `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` | Already committed, 8 tests |
| `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` | Already committed, 5 new helpers |

### Files NOT to touch

- All backend files — E2E-only story
- All frontend source files — no component changes
- Frontend unit test files — no changes needed
- `journey-first-time-user.spec.ts` — Journey 1, no changes

### Previous Story Intelligence

**From Story 3.4 (Task Deletion UI):**
- Frontend tests: 59/59 passing
- Delete × button uses `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"` — used by E2E `deleteTodo` helper
- Toggle uses `role="checkbox"` with `aria-checked` — used by E2E `expectTodoCompleted/Active`

**From QA Automation (Quinn):**
- Generated 8 Journey 2 tests covering all lifecycle scenarios
- All tests use `waitForResponse` to ensure API calls complete before assertions
- Uses `uniqueText()` pattern to avoid test data collisions
- E2E total: 17 tests (9 Journey 1 + 8 Journey 2)

### Git Intelligence

**Commit pattern:** `feat: Story X.Y: Description`
**Expected commit:** `feat: Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests`
**Recent:** `965dbd4` already committed QA-generated tests

### Scope Boundaries

- This is an **E2E test verification story** — no feature implementation
- Do NOT modify frontend or backend source code
- Do NOT add tests for Epic 4 features (empty state, loading, errors) — that's Story 4.5
- Do NOT add accessibility E2E tests — that's Story 5.4

### Library & Framework Requirements

| Library | Version | Usage |
|---------|---------|-------|
| `@playwright/test` | v1.58.x | E2E test framework |

**IMPORTANT:** Do NOT install any new dependencies.

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.5] — acceptance criteria (4 ACs, verified 1:1)
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Journey 2] — returning user journey scenarios
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Testing] — Playwright E2E covering all user journeys
- [Source: bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts] — existing Journey 2 tests (8 tests)
- [Source: bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts] — shared test helpers
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/tests/test-summary.md] — QA automation summary

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context)

### Debug Log References

- Task 1: Ran journey-returning-user.spec.ts — 8/8 pass
- Task 1: Cross-referenced all 4 ACs against 8 tests — full coverage confirmed
- Task 1: Visual gap analysis — CSS tested at component level, semantic at E2E level, no gap
- Task 2: Verified expectTodoCompleted uses aria-checked="true" — correct semantic assertion
- Task 2: Assessed CSS assertions — component tests cover line-through/italic/text-text-secondary, E2E adding them would duplicate and create brittleness
- Task 3: Full E2E suite — 17/17 pass (one flake on first run due to cache invalidation timing, 17/17 on retry; CI retries config handles this)
- Task 3: Frontend unit suite — 59/59 pass, zero regressions

### Completion Notes List

- This was a verification story — QA agent (Quinn) had already generated all Journey 2 E2E tests
- Verified 8 E2E tests map 1:1 to all 4 ACs
- Visual assertion gap assessed: AC1's "strikethrough + italic + dimmed" is tested at component level (TodoItem.test.tsx:38-43); E2E tests verify semantic state via aria-checked, which is the correct testing pyramid approach
- One flaky test observed: "completed state persists after page refresh" — timing between PATCH response and cache invalidation/refetch. Playwright auto-retry and CI retries config (retries: 2) handle this. Not a code bug.
- No files created or modified — all tests were pre-existing from QA automation
- Total E2E tests: 17 (9 Journey 1 + 8 Journey 2)
- Total frontend unit tests: 59

### Change Log

- 2026-04-10: Story 3.5 verified — all Journey 2 E2E tests confirmed against ACs, visual gap documented, 17/17 E2E + 59/59 unit pass

### File List

**Verified (no changes — pre-existing from QA automation):**
- `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` — 8 Journey 2 E2E tests (committed at `965dbd4`)
- `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` — 5 new helpers: getTodoItem, toggleTodo, deleteTodo, expectTodoCompleted, expectTodoActive

# Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests

Status: done

## Story

As a user,
I want to complete, uncomplete, and delete tasks in a full end-to-end flow,
So that the entire task lifecycle is verified across the full stack.

## Acceptance Criteria (BDD)

1. **Given** the full stack is running with existing tasks
   **When** a user completes a task
   **Then** the task shows strikethrough + italic + dimmed color
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the completed state

2. **Given** a completed task exists
   **When** a user clicks the task to uncomplete it
   **Then** the task returns to active visual state
   **And** refreshing the page preserves the active state

3. **Given** a task exists
   **When** a user deletes it via the `×` button
   **Then** the task disappears from the list
   **And** refreshing the page confirms the task is permanently gone

4. **Given** the E2E tests are run
   **When** the returning user journey (Journey 2) is executed
   **Then** all lifecycle scenarios (complete, uncomplete, delete, persistence) pass

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Verify existing Journey 2 E2E tests cover all ACs (AC: #1, #2, #3, #4)
  - [x] 1.1: Run `journey-returning-user.spec.ts` and confirm all 8 tests pass — 8/8 pass
  - [x] 1.2: Cross-reference each AC against test assertions — all 4 ACs mapped to 8 tests
  - [x] 1.3: Identify any AC gaps — visual CSS tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level; no gap requiring additional E2E assertions

- [x] Task 2: Add visual assertion for completed state if missing (AC: #1)
  - [x] 2.1: Verify `expectTodoCompleted` checks `aria-checked="true"` (semantic completion) — confirmed
  - [x] 2.2: Assess whether CSS visual assertions needed — component tests cover CSS classes (line-through, italic, text-text-secondary); E2E covers semantic state + persistence
  - [x] 2.3: No additional E2E CSS assertions needed — layered testing strategy is sound, adding CSS checks would duplicate component coverage and create brittle tests

- [x] Task 3: Run full E2E regression suite (AC: #4)
  - [x] 3.1: Run all Playwright tests — Journey 1 + Journey 2 — 17/17 pass (one flake on first run, 17/17 on retry)
  - [x] 3.2: Run frontend unit test suite — 59/59 pass, zero regressions

## Dev Notes

### CRITICAL: Tests Already Exist

The QA agent (Quinn) generated the Journey 2 E2E tests during this sprint session. The tests are:
- **File:** `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` (8 tests)
- **Helpers added:** `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` (+5 new helpers)
- **Status:** Already committed (`965dbd4`), all 17/17 E2E tests passing

**This story is a verification and gap-fill story, NOT a write-from-scratch story.** The dev agent should:
1. Verify existing tests map to ACs
2. Fill any gaps
3. Run full regression

### Existing E2E Test → AC Mapping

| Test | AC |
|------|-----|
| `clicking a task marks it as completed with visual distinction` | AC1 |
| `completed state persists after page refresh` | AC1 |
| `clicking a completed task reverts it to active visual state` | AC2 |
| `uncompleted (active) state persists after page refresh` | AC2 |
| `clicking × removes the task from the list` | AC3 |
| `deleted task is permanently gone after page refresh` | AC3 |
| `deletion is immediate — no confirmation dialog` | AC3 (bonus) |
| `create, complete, uncomplete, and delete in sequence` | AC4 |

### Visual Assertion Gap Analysis

AC1 says "strikethrough + italic + dimmed color" — the existing `expectTodoCompleted` helper checks `aria-checked="true"` which is the **semantic** completion signal. The **visual** CSS classes (`line-through italic text-text-secondary`) are already tested in component tests (`TodoItem.test.tsx` lines 38-43). E2E tests typically assert on user-visible outcomes, not implementation details. The `aria-checked` attribute is the accessibility-level truth of completion state.

**Recommended approach:** Document that visual CSS is tested at component level, semantic state at E2E level. No additional visual assertions needed unless the reviewer disagrees.

### Test Helpers Available

From `e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts`:
- `addTodo(page, text)` — fill input + press Enter
- `getTodoItem(page, text)` — locate by `role="checkbox"` + text filter
- `toggleTodo(page, text)` — click the todo item
- `deleteTodo(page, text)` — click × via `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"`
- `expectTodoCompleted(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="true"`
- `expectTodoActive(page, text)` — assert `aria-checked="false"`
- `expectTodoVisible(page, text)` — assert visible
- `getTodoCount(page)` — count items
- `getTodoTextAtPosition(page, position)` — get text at index

### Files to VERIFY (already exist)

| File | Status |
|------|--------|
| `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` | Already committed, 8 tests |
| `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` | Already committed, 5 new helpers |

### Files NOT to touch

- All backend files — E2E-only story
- All frontend source files — no component changes
- Frontend unit test files — no changes needed
- `journey-first-time-user.spec.ts` — Journey 1, no changes

### Previous Story Intelligence

**From Story 3.4 (Task Deletion UI):**
- Frontend tests: 59/59 passing
- Delete × button uses `aria-label="Delete task: [text]"` — used by E2E `deleteTodo` helper
- Toggle uses `role="checkbox"` with `aria-checked` — used by E2E `expectTodoCompleted/Active`

**From QA Automation (Quinn):**
- Generated 8 Journey 2 tests covering all lifecycle scenarios
- All tests use `waitForResponse` to ensure API calls complete before assertions
- Uses `uniqueText()` pattern to avoid test data collisions
- E2E total: 17 tests (9 Journey 1 + 8 Journey 2)

### Git Intelligence

**Commit pattern:** `feat: Story X.Y: Description`
**Expected commit:** `feat: Story 3.5: Task Lifecycle E2E Tests`
**Recent:** `965dbd4` already committed QA-generated tests

### Scope Boundaries

- This is an **E2E test verification story** — no feature implementation
- Do NOT modify frontend or backend source code
- Do NOT add tests for Epic 4 features (empty state, loading, errors) — that's Story 4.5
- Do NOT add accessibility E2E tests — that's Story 5.4

### Library & Framework Requirements

| Library | Version | Usage |
|---------|---------|-------|
| `@playwright/test` | v1.58.x | E2E test framework |

**IMPORTANT:** Do NOT install any new dependencies.

### References

- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Story 3.5] — acceptance criteria (4 ACs, verified 1:1)
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md#Journey 2] — returning user journey scenarios
- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Testing] — Playwright E2E covering all user journeys
- [Source: bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts] — existing Journey 2 tests (8 tests)
- [Source: bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts] — shared test helpers
- [Source: _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/tests/test-summary.md] — QA automation summary

## Dev Agent Record

### Agent Model Used

Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context)

### Debug Log References

- Task 1: Ran journey-returning-user.spec.ts — 8/8 pass
- Task 1: Cross-referenced all 4 ACs against 8 tests — full coverage confirmed
- Task 1: Visual gap analysis — CSS tested at component level, semantic at E2E level, no gap
- Task 2: Verified expectTodoCompleted uses aria-checked="true" — correct semantic assertion
- Task 2: Assessed CSS assertions — component tests cover line-through/italic/text-text-secondary, E2E adding them would duplicate and create brittleness
- Task 3: Full E2E suite — 17/17 pass (one flake on first run due to cache invalidation timing, 17/17 on retry; CI retries config handles this)
- Task 3: Frontend unit suite — 59/59 pass, zero regressions

### Completion Notes List

- This was a verification story — QA agent (Quinn) had already generated all Journey 2 E2E tests
- Verified 8 E2E tests map 1:1 to all 4 ACs
- Visual assertion gap assessed: AC1's "strikethrough + italic + dimmed" is tested at component level (TodoItem.test.tsx:38-43); E2E tests verify semantic state via aria-checked, which is the correct testing pyramid approach
- One flaky test observed: "completed state persists after page refresh" — timing between PATCH response and cache invalidation/refetch. Playwright auto-retry and CI retries config (retries: 2) handle this. Not a code bug.
- No files created or modified — all tests were pre-existing from QA automation
- Total E2E tests: 17 (9 Journey 1 + 8 Journey 2)
- Total frontend unit tests: 59

### Change Log

- 2026-04-10: Story 3.5 verified — all Journey 2 E2E tests confirmed against ACs, visual gap documented, 17/17 E2E + 59/59 unit pass

### File List

**Verified (no changes — pre-existing from QA automation):**
- `bmad-todo/e2e/tests/journey-returning-user.spec.ts` — 8 Journey 2 E2E tests (committed at `965dbd4`)
- `bmad-todo/e2e/fixtures/test-helpers.ts` — 5 new helpers: getTodoItem, toggleTodo, deleteTodo, expectTodoCompleted, expectTodoActive

# STATUS DEFINITIONS:
# ==================
# Epic Status:
#   - backlog: Epic not yet started
#   - in-progress: Epic actively being worked on
#   - done: All stories in epic completed
#
# Epic Status Transitions:
#   - backlog → in-progress: Automatically when first story is created (via create-story)
#   - in-progress → done: Manually when all stories reach 'done' status
#
# Story Status:
#   - backlog: Story only exists in epic file
#   - ready-for-dev: Story file created in stories folder
#   - in-progress: Developer actively working on implementation
#   - review: Ready for code review (via Dev's code-review workflow)
#   - done: Story completed
#
# Retrospective Status:
#   - optional: Can be completed but not required
#   - done: Retrospective has been completed
#
# WORKFLOW NOTES:
# ===============
# - Epic transitions to 'in-progress' automatically when first story is created
# - Stories can be worked in parallel if team capacity allows
# - SM typically creates next story after previous one is 'done' to incorporate learnings
# - Dev moves story to 'review', then runs code-review (fresh context, different LLM recommended)

# Retrospective - Epic Completion Review Workflow
name: "retrospective"
description: 'Post-epic review to extract lessons and assess success. Use when the user says "run a retrospective" or "lets retro the epic [epic]"'

config_source: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/config.yaml"
user_name: "{config_source}:user_name"
communication_language: "{config_source}:communication_language"
user_skill_level: "{config_source}:user_skill_level"
document_output_language: "{config_source}:document_output_language"
date: system-generated
planning_artifacts: "{config_source}:planning_artifacts"
implementation_artifacts: "{config_source}:implementation_artifacts"
project_context: "**/project-context.md"

installed_path: "{project-root}/_bmad/bmm/workflows/4-implementation/retrospective"
template: false
instructions: "{installed_path}/instructions.md"

required_inputs:
  - agent_manifest: "{project-root}/_bmad/_config/agent-manifest.csv"

# Smart input file references - handles both whole docs and sharded docs
# Priority: Whole document first, then sharded version
# Strategy: SELECTIVE LOAD - only load the completed epic and relevant retrospectives
input_file_patterns:
  epics:
    description: "The completed epic for retrospective"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*.md"
    sharded_index: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*/index.md"
    sharded_single: "{planning_artifacts}/*epic*/epic-{{epic_num}}.md"
    load_strategy: "SELECTIVE_LOAD"
  previous_retrospective:
    description: "Previous epic's retrospective (optional)"
    pattern: "{implementation_artifacts}/**/epic-{{prev_epic_num}}-retro-*.md"
    load_strategy: "SELECTIVE_LOAD"
  architecture:
    description: "System architecture for context"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*architecture*.md"
    sharded: "{planning_artifacts}/*architecture*/*.md"
    load_strategy: "FULL_LOAD"
  prd:
    description: "Product requirements for context"
    whole: "{planning_artifacts}/*prd*.md"
    sharded: "{planning_artifacts}/*prd*/*.md"
    load_strategy: "FULL_LOAD"
  document_project:
    description: "Brownfield project documentation (optional)"
    sharded: "{planning_artifacts}/*.md"
    load_strategy: "INDEX_GUIDED"

# Required files
sprint_status_file: "{implementation_artifacts}/sprint-status.yaml"
